| Web search results for query: cross-platform design system visual consistency web iOS Android React Native Next.js 2024 |  | Links:<br/>1. [Cross-Platform Consistency with Design Systems](https://developerux.com/2025/06/18/cross-platform-consistency-with-design-systems/)<br/>2. [Maintaining design system component consistency when integrating new features across multiple platforms such as web, iOS, and Android is critical for delivering a seamless user experience and cohesive brand presence. Below are actionable strategies and best practices to help development teams uphold design consistency at scale.](http://www.zigpoll.com/content/how-can-we-ensure-the-design-system-components-maintain-consistency-when-developers-integrate-new-features-across-multiple-platforms)<br/>3. [Universal styling across platforms](https://app.studyraid.com/en/read/12413/400749/universal-styling-across-platforms)<br/>4. [Creating a Cross-Platform Design System for React and React Native with Bit](https://bit.dev/blog/creating-a-cross-platform-design-system-for-react-and-react-native-with-bit-l7i3qgmw/)<br/>5. [Platform-Specific Code · React Native](https://reactnative.dev/docs/next/platform-specific-code)<br/><br/># Cross-Platform Design System for Visual Consistency (2024)<br/><br/>## Core Approach: Design Tokens & Shared Guidelines<br/><br/>Design tokens serve as the foundation for cross-platform consistency, acting as a "single source of truth" for visual elements like colors, typography, spacing, and animations.[1] These centralized, platform-agnostic variables ensure the same design decisions apply across iOS, Android, and web.[1] Tools like Style Dictionary and Theo convert tokens into platform-specific formats (CSS, XML, Swift enums), with updates automatically propagating across all platforms.[2]<br/><br/>## Component Libraries & Frameworks<br/><br/>**Cross-platform frameworks** like React Native, Flutter, and Tamagui enable code reuse while respecting platform conventions.[2][3] Tamagui's compiler-first architecture transforms platform-agnostic styles into optimized code at build time—generating React Native StyleSheet objects for mobile and atomic CSS for web.[3] React and React Native teams can share design tokens, types, and hooks through monorepo architectures (using tools like Nx and Bit), while maintaining platform-specific UI implementations.[4]<br/><br/>## Implementation Best Practices<br/><br/>**Version Control & Documentation**: Use semantic versioning and CI/CD pipelines to automate synchronization; distribute components through package managers.[2]<br/><br/>**Platform-Aware Design**: Balance consistent visual identity with native feel by creating platform-specific wrappers around shared logic.[2] React Native's Platform module enables conditional rendering for iOS/Android differences.[5]<br/><br/>**Tools & Governance**: Storybook, Zeroheight, and Percy streamline design, documentation, and testing. Regular audits and team collaboration prevent style drift.[1]<br/><br/>Design systems reduce development time by up to 50% and double user satisfaction through this standardized, multi-platform approach.[1] |

</details>
</tool-use>

| Web search results for query: Style Dictionary design tokens cross-platform JSON single source of truth 2024 |  | Links:<br/>1. [style-dictionary](https://npmjs.com/style-dictionary)<br/>2. [Style Dictionary \| Style Dictionary](https://styledictionary.com/)<br/>3. [Package Structure \| Style Dictionary](https://styledictionary.com/info/package_structure/)<br/>4. [Configuration \| Style Dictionary](https://styledictionary.com/reference/config/)<br/>5. [Design Tokens](https://styledictionary.com/info/tokens/)<br/><br/># Style Dictionary: Cross-Platform Design Tokens & Single Source of Truth<br/><br/>Style Dictionary is a tool that enables teams to define design tokens once and export them across multiple platforms.[1] It uses JSON-based design token files as a single source of truth, allowing automatic generation of style definitions for iOS, Android, CSS, JavaScript, HTML, Sketch files, and other platforms.[1][2]<br/><br/>## Key Features<br/><br/>**Configuration-Driven Approach**: Style Dictionary operates through a `config.json` file that specifies where design tokens are located and how they should be transformed and formatted for each target platform.[4] The configuration can be written in JSON, JSONC, JSON5, or JavaScript (ES modules), with JavaScript offering additional flexibility for programmatic configuration.[4]<br/><br/>**Design Token Format**: Design tokens are defined in JSON files with a `value` attribute and optional metadata like `type`, `comment`, and custom attributes.[5] Style Dictionary supports both its own format and the Design Tokens Community Group (DTCG) specification, making it forward-compatible with industry standards.[2]<br/><br/>**Cross-Platform Output**: The tool transforms tokens into platform-specific formats through configurable transforms and formats. Teams can define tokens once and automatically generate outputs for different technologies without manual duplication.[1][4]<br/><br/>## Current Status (2024)<br/><br/>The latest version is 5.2.0,[1] with Version 4 representing a significant update. Style Dictionary can be used as a CLI tool via npm or as a Node.js module for programmatic control.[1][4] An interactive playground is available at style-dictionary-play.dev for experimentation.[1]<br/><br/>The tool maintains optional asset management capabilities, allowing images, fonts, and other assets to be centralized in the style dictionary as part of the single source of truth.[3] |

</details>
</tool-use>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Comparing design systems**

I'm analyzing the differences between web and mobile design systems, focusing on spacing, typography, and border implementations. Mobile uses 8px spacing values as numbers, while web uses CSS strings for spacing, shadows, and typography. 

**Evaluating design token strategies**

I'm weighing JSON tokens with build steps against shared TypeScript tokens with platform adapters. I'm choosing Option B: shared canonical values with platform-specific adapters for minimal production code. I'm exporting semantic values from the shared package and letting platform packages handle their own formatting. </details></think>

---



---


Here’s what changed for cross-platform visual coherence:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. **Shared design system** (`@waqup/shared/theme`)

- **`colors.ts`** – Single palette shared across platforms
- **`tokens.ts`** – Canonical numeric tokens for spacing, borderRadius, typography, shadows, layout
- **`types.ts`** – Shared `Theme`, `ThemeVariables`
- **`themes.ts`** – Theme generation logic (WCAG contrast, gradients, mystical effects) with 6 themes

### 2. **Platform adapters**

- **Web** (`packages/web/src/theme/`): ~60 lines – converts numbers to px strings, builds shadow CSS, keeps `backdrop-filter` for glass
- **Mobile** (`packages/mobile/src/theme/`): ~80 lines – uses numbers directly, builds RN `shadowOffset`/`shadowOpacity`/`elevation` objects

### 3. **Code reduction**

- **Before**: ~500+ lines per platform (colors, spacing, typography, borders, shadows, themes, glass, etc.)
- **After**: ~120 lines shared + ~140 lines across both platforms
- **Removed**: ~600+ duplicated lines

### 4. **Approach**

- Canonical numbers in shared; each platform converts only where needed (e.g. CSS px strings vs RN numbers)
- Semantic shadow tokens `{ y, blur, opacity }` → Web: CSS box-shadow string; RN: `shadowOffset` / `shadowRadius` / `elevation`
- Single theme logic: one generator for web and mobile

### 5. **Files touched**

- **Shared**: Added `packages/shared/src/theme/` (colors, tokens, types, themes, index)
- **Web**: Replaced old theme folder with `format.ts`, `glass.ts`, `ThemeProvider.tsx`, `index.ts`; removed 8 duplicated theme files
- **Mobile**: Same layout; removed 8 duplicated theme files
- **Imports**: Updated all `@/theme/design-tokens` imports to `@/theme`

### 6. **Docs**

- Added `docs/04-reference/07-design-system-cross-platform.md` describing the design system architecture

`npm run type-check` passes for the monorepo, so web, iOS, and Android now share one design token source with small platform-specific adapters.

          }
        },
        "contents": "# Multi-Platform Strategy - waQup\n\n**Purpose**: Define strategy for mobile apps (iOS/Android) and web platform (desktop browsers) with Revolut-level quality standards\n\n---\n\n## Platform Overview\n\nwaQup will be available on **two platforms**, developed **simultaneously from scratch**:\n\n1. **Mobile Apps** (iOS &amp; Android) - React Native + Expo\n2. **Web Platform** (Desktop Browsers) - Next.js (Chrome-first, PWA support)\n\n**Development Strategy**: Both platforms developed in parallel, tested simultaneously, ensuring consistency and quality across all platforms from day one.\n\n**Note**: Desktop functionality is provided via the web platform (Next.js), which works on desktop browsers with PWA support for an app-like experience.\n\n---\n\n## Platform-Specific Behaviors &amp; Differences\n\n### 1. Mobile Apps (iOS &amp; Android)\n\n**Primary Use Case**: On-the-go practice, background audio, notifications\n\n**Key Behaviors**:\n- **Background Audio**: Audio continues playing when app is backgrounded\n- **Lock Screen Controls**: Media controls on lock screen\n- **Notifications**: Push notifications for practice reminders\n- **Offline Mode**: Download content for offline use\n- **Touch Gestures**: Swipe, pinch, tap interactions\n- **Native Feel**: Platform-specific UI patterns (iOS vs Material Design)\n- **App Store Distribution**: Native app store presence\n\n**Technical Stack**:\n- React Native + Expo SDK 54\n- Native modules for audio (expo-av)\n- Push notifications (expo-notifications)\n- Deep linking support\n\n**Quality Standards**:\n- **Performance**: &lt; 2s app launch, &lt; 300ms screen transitions\n- **Animations**: 60fps smooth animations (react-native-reanimated)\n- **Touch Response**: &lt; 100ms touch feedback\n- **Audio Latency**: &lt; 50ms audio start delay\n- **Battery**: Optimized background audio, minimal battery drain\n\n---\n\n### 2. Web Platform (Desktop Browsers)\n\n**Primary Use Case**: Desktop access, quick access, sharing, discovery, no installation required\n\n**Key Behaviors**:\n- **Progressive Web App (PWA)**: Installable as desktop app, offline support\n- **Desktop Optimized**: Full keyboard navigation, desktop screen sizes",

        "contents": "**From Phase 1**: All three platforms set up simultaneously with shared codebase\n\n**Shared Codebase** (Monorepo Structure):\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nwaqup-app/\n├── packages/\n│   ├── shared/              # Shared code (business logic)\n│   │   ├── services/        # API services\n│   │   ├── stores/          # Zustand stores\n│   │   ├── types/           # TypeScript types\n│   │   ├── utils/           # Utilities\n│   │   └── schemas/         # Validation schemas\n│   │\n│   ├── mobile/              # React Native app\n│   │   ├── app/             # App entry\n│   │   ├── components/      # React Native components\n│   │   ├── screens/         # Mobile screens\n│   │   └── native/          # Native modules\n│   │\n│   ├── desktop/             # Electron/Tauri app\n│   │   ├── src/             # Desktop app source\n│   │   ├── components/      # Desktop-specific components\n│   │   ├── windows/         # Window management\n│   │   └── native/          # Native integrations\n│   │\n│   └── web/                 # Next.js app\n│       ├── app/             # Next.js App Router\n│       ├── components/      # Web components\n│       └── public/          # Static assets\n│\n└── package.json            # Root workspace config\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Code Sharing Strategy**:\n- **Shared**: Business logic, state management, types, validation, utilities\n- **Platform-Specific**: UI components, navigation, audio, file system, notifications\n\n**Development Workflow**:\n1. **Phase 1**: Set up all three platforms simultaneously\n2. **Each Phase**: Implement feature on all three platforms in parallel\n3. **Testing**: Test all three platforms simultaneously\n4. **Quality**: Ensure consistency across all platforms\n\n---\n\n## Quality Testing in Design Phase\n\n### Phase 2: Design System &amp; UI Foundation - Quality Testing\n\n**New Step 2.5: Quality Testing &amp; Platform Verification**",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/02-mobile/02-architecture.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 100
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Architecture - waQup Mobile App\n\n**Status**: ✅ Defined | **Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n\n## Overview\n\nMobile app architecture optimized for React Native + Expo, sharing Supabase backend with web app.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nMobile App (React Native)\n    ↓\nSupabase Backend (PostgreSQL, Auth, Storage, Realtime)\n    ↓\nExternal Services (ElevenLabs, OpenAI, Stripe)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## App Layers\n\n### 1. Presentation Layer\n**Screens** (&#96;src/screens/&#96;):\n- Auth (Login, Signup, ForgotPassword)\n- Main (Home, Library, Create, Profile)\n- Content (Detail screens, Creation flows)\n\n**Components** (&#96;src/components/&#96;):\n- UI primitives (Button, Input, Card)\n- Features (AudioPlayer, ContentCard)\n- Layouts (Header, TabBar)\n\n### 2. State Management\n**Global State** (Zustand - &#96;src/stores/&#96;):\n- Auth store (user, session)\n- UI store (modals, loading)\n- Preferences store\n\n**Server State** (React Query - &#96;src/hooks/&#96;):\n- Content queries\n- User profile\n- Practice sessions\n- Real-time subscriptions\n\n### 3. Service Layer\n**API Services** (&#96;src/services/&#96;):\n- Supabase client\n- Content API\n- Audio API\n- Payment API\n- Auth API\n\n**Storage** (&#96;src/services/storage/&#96;):\n- Audio upload/download\n- File management\n\n### 4. Navigation Layer\n**Structure** (&#96;src/navigation/&#96;):\n- RootNavigator (Auth vs Main)\n- AuthNavigator (Stack)\n- MainNavigator (Bottom Tabs)\n- ContentNavigator (Stack)\n\n---\n\n## Data Flow\n\n### Content Creation\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser Input → Validation → Service → Supabase → External API → Process → Update State → UI\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Audio Playback\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nUser Taps Play → Audio Service → Load from Storage → Play → Track Progress → Record Session\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Key Decisions\n\n### Offline-First\n- Cache content locally (React Query)\n- Store audio files locally\n- Queue actions when offline\n- Sync when online\n\n### Real-Time Updates\n- Supabase Realtime subscriptions\n- Update React Query cache automatically\n- Live UI updates\n\n### Audio Handling\n- Background audio support\n- Handle interruptions gracefully\n- Cache audio files locally\n- Stream from CDN\n\n### Performance\n- Lazy load screens\n- Optimize images",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "### 3. Service Layer",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "**API Services** (&#96;src/services/&#96;):",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "- Supabase client",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "- Content API",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "- Audio API",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "- Payment API",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "- Auth API",
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "text": "**Storage** (&#96;src/services/storage/&#96;):",
            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "- Audio upload/download",
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "text": "- File management",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "text": "### 4. Navigation Layer",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "**Structure** (&#96;src/navigation/&#96;):",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "- RootNavigator (Auth vs Main)",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "- AuthNavigator (Stack)",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "- MainNavigator (Bottom Tabs)",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "- ContentNavigator (Stack)",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "## Data Flow",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "### Content Creation",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "User Input → Validation → Service → Supabase → External API → Process → Update State → UI",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "### Audio Playback",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "User Taps Play → Audio Service → Load from Storage → Play → Track Progress → Record Session",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "## Key Decisions",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "### Offline-First",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "- Cache content locally (React Query)",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "- Store audio files locally",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "- Queue actions when offline",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "- Sync when online",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "### Real-Time Updates",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "- Supabase Realtime subscriptions",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "- Update React Query cache automatically",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "- Live UI updates",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "### Audio Handling",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "- Background audio support",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "- Handle interruptions gracefully",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "- Cache audio files locally",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "- Stream from CDN",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "### Performance",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "- Lazy load screens",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "- Optimize images",
            "lineNumber": 100
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.3496860861778259
    },

decisions\n│   ├── 02-architecture.md      # Mobile architecture\n│   └── 03-implementation.md    # Mobile implementation guide\n│\n├── 03-platforms/               # Multi-platform strategy &amp; optimization\n│   ├── README.md               # Platform docs overview\n│   ├── 01-multi-platform-strategy.md  # Mobile + Web strategy\n│   └── 02-browser-optimization-strategy.md  # Chrome-first browser strategy\n│\n└── 04-reference/               # Reference guides\n    ├── 01-showcase-access.md   # Component showcase (Web &amp;       "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/02-mobile/01-technology-stack.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 79
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Technology Stack - waQup Mobile App\n\n**Status**: ✅ Finalized | **Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n\n## Quick Reference\n\n| Category | Technology | Version |\n|----------|-----------|---------|\n| **Framework** | React Native + Expo | SDK 54 |\n| **Language** | TypeScript | 5.6+ |\n| **Navigation** | React Navigation | v7 |\n| **State** | Zustand + React Query | v4 + v5 |\n| **Backend** | Supabase | Latest |\n| **Audio** | expo-av | ~14.0 |\n| **TTS** | ElevenLabs | Professional Voice Cloning |\n| **AI** | OpenAI | GPT-4o-mini / GPT-4 |\n| **Payments** | Stripe React Native | Latest |\n| **Forms** | react-hook-form + zod | Latest |\n| **Animations** | react-native-reanimated | v3 |\n\n---\n\n## Core Decisions\n\n### React Native + Expo ✅\n**Why**: Cross-platform, cost-effective (~50% vs native), excellent Supabase support, OTA updates, App Store ready\n\n**Key Packages**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"expo\": \"~54.0.0\",\n  \"react-native\": \"0.81.5\",\n  \"@react-navigation/native\": \"^7.1.0\",\n  \"@react-navigation/native-stack\": \"^7.1.0\",\n  \"@react-navigation/bottom-tabs\": \"^7.1.0\"\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Supabase (Backend) ✅\n**Why**: Same database as web app, cost-effective (~$50-100/month), built-in auth/storage/realtime\n\n**Configuration**:\n- Use &#96;@supabase/supabase-js&#96; with AsyncStorage\n- Set &#96;detectSessionInUrl: false&#96; for mobile\n- Enable real-time subscriptions\n\n### Audio: ElevenLabs + expo-av ✅\n**TTS**: ElevenLabs Professional Voice Cloning\n- Enterprise-grade (10,000+ businesses)\n- Best for long-form content (10-30 min)\n- 70+ languages\n- Professional quality\n\n**Playback**: expo-av\n- Native audio playback\n- Background audio support\n- Handles interruptions\n\n### AI: OpenAI ✅\n**Conversations**: GPT-4o-mini (75% cost reduction)\n- Fast, cost-effective\n- Excellent for question generation\n- Prompt caching support\n\n**Scripts**: GPT-4 (Batch API for 50% savings)\n- Highest quality\n- Type-specific structures\n- Batch processing for background\n\n### Payments: Stripe React Native SDK ✅\n- Official SDK\n- Native payment UI\n- Apple Pay / Google Pay\n- Secure (PCI compliant)\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure",

      "codeBlock": {

          "endPosition": {

          }
        },
        "contents": "**Key Behaviors**:\n- **Progressive Web App (PWA)**: Installable as desktop app, offline support\n- **Desktop Optimized**: Full keyboard navigation, desktop screen sizes\n- **URL Sharing**: Direct links to content, shareable URLs\n- **SEO**: Content discoverable via search engines\n- **Cross-Platform**: Works on any device with browser\n- **No Installation Required**: Instant access, no app store approval\n- **Embedding**: Content can be embedded on other sites\n- **Marketplace Discovery**: Web-first marketplace browsing\n- **Desktop Features**: Keyboard shortcuts, multi-tab support, browser extensions\n\n**Technical Stack**:\n- Next.js 16.1.6 (latest stable)\n- PWA support (service workers, manifest)\n- Web Audio API\n- Responsive design (mobile-first, desktop-optimized)\n- Tailwind CSS 4\n\n**Browser Strategy**: **Chrome-First** (see [Browser Optimization Strategy](./02-browser-optimization-strategy.md))\n- **Phase 1**: Optimize exclusively for Chrome (~80% market share)\n- **Phase 2**: Expand to Safari, Firefox, Edge post-launch\n- **Recommendation**: Recommend Chrome to users for best experience\n\n**Quality Standards**:\n- **Performance**: &lt; 2s first load (Chrome), &lt; 1s subsequent loads\n- **Lighthouse**: &gt; 95 score (Chrome optimized)\n- **Responsive**: Works on mobile, tablet, desktop browsers\n- **Offline**: PWA offline support (Chrome best support)\n- **Audio**: Web Audio API with low latency (&lt; 20ms on Chrome)\n\n---\n\n## Revolut-Level Quality Standards\n\n### Design Quality Principles\n\n**1. Micro-Interactions**\n- Every interaction has immediate visual feedback\n- Smooth animations (60fps) for all transitions\n- Haptic feedback on mobile (where appropriate)\n- Loading states are informative, not just spinners\n\n**2. Consistency**\n- Same design language across all platforms\n- Platform-appropriate adaptations (iOS vs Android vs Desktop)\n- Consistent spacing, typography, colors\n- Unified component library (with platform variants)\n\n**3. Performance**\n- **Mobile**: &lt; 2s launch, &lt; 300ms transitions, 60fps animations\n- **Desktop**: &lt; 1s launch, instant interactions, smooth window management\n- **Web**: &lt; 3s first load, &lt; 1s subsequent, &gt; 90 Lighthouse score",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "# Implementation Guide - waQup Mobile App\n\n**Status**: 📝 Active | **Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n\n## Quick Start\n\n1. **Follow Roadmap**: &#96;../../rebuild-roadmap/01-planning/01-roadmap.md&#96;\n2. **Update Changelog**: &#96;../../rebuild-roadmap/03-tracking/01-changelog.md&#96; after each step\n3. **Reference This Guide**: For patterns and gotchas\n\n---\n\n## Essential Patterns\n\n### Supabase Connection\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';\nimport AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';\n\nexport const supabase = createClient(\n  process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,\n  process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!,\n  {\n    auth: {\n      storage: AsyncStorage,\n      autoRefreshToken: true,\n      persistSession: true,\n      detectSessionInUrl: false, // Important for mobile\n    },\n  }\n);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Audio Playback (expo-av)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { Audio } from 'expo-av';\n\nconst { sound } = await Audio.Sound.createAsync(\n  { uri: audioUrl },\n  { shouldPlay: true }\n);\n\n// Background audio config in app.json\n{\n  \"expo\": {\n    \"plugins\": [[\"expo-av\", {\n      \"microphonePermission\": \"Allow waQup to access your microphone.\"\n    }]],\n    \"ios\": {\n      \"infoPlist\": {\n        \"UIBackgroundModes\": [\"audio\"]\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### React Query Hook\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nconst { data, isLoading, error } = useQuery({\n  queryKey: ['content', contentId],\n  queryFn: () =&gt; getContent(contentId),\n  cacheTime: Infinity,\n  staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // 5 minutes\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Zustand Store\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { create } from 'zustand';\n\ninterface AuthState {\n  user: User | null;\n  setUser: (user: User | null) =&gt; void;\n}\n\nconst useAuthStore = create&lt;AuthState&gt;((set) =&gt; ({\n  user: null,\n  setUser: (user) =&gt; set({ user }),\n}));\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Forms (react-hook-form + zod)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { useForm } from 'react-hook-form';\nimport { zodResolver } from '@hookform/resolvers/zod';\nimport { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst schema = z.object({\n  email: z.string().email(),\n  password: z.string().min(8),\n});",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "- Apple Pay / Google Pay\n- Secure (PCI compliant)\n\n---\n\n## Project Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nsrc/\n├── app/              # App entry\n├── screens/          # Screen components\n├── components/       # Reusable components\n│   ├── ui/          # UI primitives\n│   └── features/    # Feature components\n├── navigation/       # Navigation config\n├── services/         # API services\n│   ├── supabase/    # Supabase client\n│   ├── api/         # API functions\n│   └── storage/     # Storage functions\n├── stores/           # Zustand stores\n├── hooks/            # React Query hooks\n├── types/            # TypeScript types\n├── utils/            # Utilities\n└── constants/        # Constants\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Key Patterns\n\n### Supabase Connection\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';\nimport AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';\n\nexport const supabase = createClient(\n  process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!,\n  process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!,\n  {\n    auth: {\n      storage: AsyncStorage,\n      autoRefreshToken: true,\n      persistSession: true,\n      detectSessionInUrl: false,\n    },\n  }\n);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### React Query Hook\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nconst { data, isLoading } = useQuery({\n  queryKey: ['content', id],\n  queryFn: () =&gt; getContent(id),\n  cacheTime: Infinity,\n  staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000,\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Zustand Store\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nconst useAuthStore = create&lt;AuthState&gt;((set) =&gt; ({\n  user: null,\n  setUser: (user) =&gt; set({ user }),\n}));\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Cost Estimates (10K Users)\n\n- OpenAI TTS: ~$750/month\n- OpenAI LLM: ~$50-200/month\n- Supabase: ~$25/month\n- Storage: ~$2.50/month\n- Vercel: ~$20/month\n- **Total**: ~$850-1,000/month\n\n**Optimizations**:\n- GPT-4o-mini for conversations (75% savings)\n- Batch API for scripts (50% savings)\n- Prompt caching (up to 50% additional)\n- Vercel AI SDK caching (30-50% savings)\n\n---\n\n## References\n\n- **Full Details**: See &#96;../../../docs/internal/05-system-architecture.md&#96;\n- **Audio Details**: See &#96;../../../docs/internal/audio-generation.md&#96;",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/03-platforms/01-multi-platform-strategy.md",

        "contents": "- **Desktop**: &lt; 1s launch, instant interactions, smooth window management\n- **Web**: &lt; 3s first load, &lt; 1s subsequent, &gt; 90 Lighthouse score\n\n**4. Error Handling**\n- Graceful error states (not just error messages)\n- Retry mechanisms with clear feedback\n- Offline mode with clear indicators\n- Network error handling with retry options\n\n**5. Accessibility**\n- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance\n- Keyboard navigation on all platforms\n- Screen reader support\n- High contrast mode support\n- Font scaling support\n\n**6. Polish**\n- No janky animations\n- Smooth scrolling everywhere\n- Proper loading states\n- Empty states with helpful guidance\n- Onboarding that's helpful, not annoying\n\n---\n\n## Platform-Specific Adaptations\n\n### Mobile Adaptations\n\n**iOS**:\n- Native iOS design patterns (NavigationController, TabBar)\n- iOS-specific animations (spring physics)\n- Haptic feedback (Haptic Engine)\n- Face ID / Touch ID integration\n- iOS share sheet integration\n- Siri Shortcuts support (future)\n\n**Android**:\n- Material Design 3 components\n- Android-specific animations (Material Motion)\n- Haptic feedback (Vibration API)\n- Android share sheet integration\n- Android Auto support (future)\n- Widget support (future)\n\n### Desktop Adaptations\n\n**macOS**:\n- Native macOS menu bar\n- macOS-style window controls\n- Touch Bar support (if available)\n- Spotlight integration (future)\n- macOS notifications\n\n**Windows**:\n- Windows-style window controls\n- Windows notifications\n- Windows taskbar integration\n- Windows 11 design language\n\n**Linux**:\n- GTK/Qt integration options\n- Linux notifications\n- Desktop environment integration\n\n### Web Adaptations\n\n**Browser-Specific**:\n- Chrome: Best PWA support\n- Safari: iOS PWA limitations handled\n- Firefox: Full support\n- Edge: Full support\n\n**Responsive Breakpoints**:\n- Mobile: &lt; 768px\n- Tablet: 768px - 1024px\n- Desktop: &gt; 1024px\n\n---\n\n## Shared Codebase Strategy\n\n### Parallel Development Architecture\n\n**From Phase 1**: All three platforms set up simultaneously with shared codebase\n\n**Shared Codebase** (Monorepo Structure):\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nwaqup-app/\n├── packages/",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "- **Desktop**: &lt; 1s launch, instant interactions, smooth window management",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Web**: &lt; 3s first load, &lt; 1s subsequent, &gt; 90 Lighthouse score",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "**4. Error Handling**",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "- Graceful error states (not just error messages)",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "- Retry mechanisms with clear feedback",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "- Offline mode with clear indicators",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "- Network error handling with retry options",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "**5. Accessibility**",
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "text": "- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance",
            "lineNumber": 114
          },
          {
            "text": "- Keyboard navigation on all platforms",
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "text": "- Screen reader support",
            "lineNumber": 116
          },
          {
            "text": "- High contrast mode support",
            "lineNumber": 117
          },
          {
            "text": "- Font scaling support",
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "text": "**6. Polish**",
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "- No janky animations",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "text": "- Smooth scrolling everywhere",
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "- Proper loading states",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "text": "- Empty states with helpful guidance",
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "- Onboarding that's helpful, not annoying",
            "lineNumber": 125
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 127
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128
          },
          {
            "text": "## Platform-Specific Adaptations",
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130
          },
          {
            "text": "### Mobile Adaptations",
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "text": "**iOS**:",
            "lineNumber": 133
          },
          {
            "text": "- Native iOS design patterns (NavigationController, TabBar)",
            "lineNumber": 134
          },
          {
            "text": "- iOS-specific animations (spring physics)",
            "lineNumber": 135
          },
          {
            "text": "- Haptic feedback (Haptic Engine)",
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "text": "- Face ID / Touch ID integration",
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {
            "text": "- iOS share sheet integration",
            "lineNumber": 138
          },
          {
            "text": "- Siri Shortcuts support (future)",
            "lineNumber": 139
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "text": "**Android**:",
            "lineNumber": 141
          },
          {
            "text": "- Material Design 3 components",
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "text": "- Android-specific animations (Material Motion)",
            "lineNumber": 143
          },
          {
            "text": "- Haptic feedback (Vibration API)",
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "text": "- Android share sheet integration",
            "lineNumber": 145
          },
          {
            "text": "- Android Auto support (future)",
            "lineNumber": 146
          },
          {
            "text": "- Widget support (future)",
            "lineNumber": 147
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148
          },
          {
            "text": "### Desktop Adaptations",
            "lineNumber": 149
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150
          },
          {
            "text": "**macOS**:",
            "lineNumber": 151
          },
          {
            "text": "- Native macOS menu bar",
            "lineNumber": 152
          },
          {
            "text": "- macOS-style window controls",
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "text": "- Touch Bar support (if available)",
            "lineNumber": 154
          },
          {
            "text": "- Spotlight integration (future)",
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "- macOS notifications",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "**Windows**:",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "text": "- Windows-style window controls",
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "text": "- Windows notifications",
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "text": "- Windows taskbar integration",
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "- Windows 11 design language",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "**Linux**:",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "text": "- GTK/Qt integration options",
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "- Linux notifications",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "text": "- Desktop environment integration",
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "text": "### Web Adaptations",
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "**Browser-Specific**:",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "- Chrome: Best PWA support",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "- Safari: iOS PWA limitations handled",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "- Firefox: Full support",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "- Edge: Full support",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "**Responsive Breakpoints**:",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "- Mobile: &lt; 768px",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "- Tablet: 768px - 1024px",
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "text": "- Desktop: &gt; 1024px",
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "text": "## Shared Codebase Strategy",
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185

          {
            "text": "### Parallel Development Architecture",

        "contents": "# Browser Optimization Strategy - Chrome-First Approach\n\n**Purpose**: Optimize for Chrome (~80% market share) initially, recommend Chrome to users, then expand to other browsers later\n\n---\n\n## Strategy Overview\n\n### Chrome-First Approach\n\n**Phase 1 (Initial Launch)**: Optimize exclusively for Chrome\n- Target: Chrome/Chromium browsers (~80% market share)\n- Recommend Chrome to users\n- Faster development and optimization\n- Best performance and feature support\n\n**Phase 2 (Expansion)**: Add support for other browsers\n- Safari (iOS/macOS)\n- Firefox\n- Edge (Chromium-based, easier)\n- Other browsers as needed\n\n---\n\n## Why Chrome First?\n\n### Market Share\n- **Chrome**: ~65-80% global market share (2025)\n- **Safari**: ~15-20% (mostly iOS/macOS)\n- **Edge**: ~5-8% (Chromium-based, similar to Chrome)\n- **Firefox**: ~3-5%\n\n### Technical Advantages\n- **Best Performance**: V8 engine, fastest JavaScript execution\n- **Best Web Audio API**: Lowest latency, best quality\n- **Best PWA Support**: Full PWA features, offline support\n- **Best Developer Tools**: Excellent debugging and profiling\n- **Latest Web Standards**: Fastest adoption of new features\n- **WebAssembly**: Best performance\n- **WebGPU**: Advanced graphics (future)\n\n### Development Benefits\n- **Faster Development**: No cross-browser compatibility issues initially\n- **Better Performance**: Optimize for one engine\n- **Easier Testing**: Test on one browser initially\n- **Faster Iteration**: No need to fix browser-specific bugs\n\n---\n\n## Chrome-Specific Optimizations\n\n### 1. Web Audio API Optimizations\n\n**Chrome Advantages**:\n- Lowest audio latency (~10-20ms)\n- Best audio quality\n- Best performance for real-time audio\n- Web Audio API v2 support\n\n**Optimizations**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\n// Chrome-specific audio optimizations\nconst audioContext = new AudioContext({\n  latencyHint: 'interactive', // Chrome optimizes this best\n  sampleRate: 48000, // Chrome supports high sample rates\n});\n\n// Use Chrome's optimized audio nodes\nconst gainNode = audioContext.createGain();\nconst analyser = audioContext.createAnalyser();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "# Platform Documentation\n\n**Purpose**: Multi-platform strategy and optimization guides for Mobile and Web platforms\n\n---\n\n## Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n03-platforms/\n├── README.md                           # This file\n├── 01-multi-platform-strategy.md       # Mobile + Web strategy\n└── 02-browser-optimization-strategy.md # Chrome-first browser strategy\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Quick Navigation\n\n### 📱 Multi-Platform Strategy\n- **[01 Multi-Platform Strategy](./01-multi-platform-strategy.md)** - Strategy for Mobile and Web platforms developed simultaneously\n\n### 🌐 Browser Optimization\n- **[02 Browser Optimization Strategy](./02-browser-optimization-strategy.md)** - Chrome-first browser optimization strategy\n\n---\n\n## Documentation Hierarchy\n\n### Mobile-Specific Docs\nSee &#96;../02-mobile/&#96; for:\n- Technology Stack (mobile)\n- Architecture (mobile)\n- Implementation (mobile)\n\n### Platform Strategy Docs\nThis folder (&#96;03-platforms/&#96;) contains:\n- Multi-platform development strategy (Mobile + Web)\n- Browser optimization strategy\n- Cross-platform considerations\n\n### Core Product Docs\nSee &#96;../01-core/&#96; for:\n- Reference to main product documentation\n\n---\n\n## How to Use\n\n1. **For Mobile Development**: See &#96;../02-mobile/&#96; (mobile-specific)\n2. **For Multi-Platform Strategy**: Read [01 Multi-Platform Strategy](./01-multi-platform-strategy.md)\n3. **For Browser Optimization**: Read [02 Browser Optimization Strategy](./02-browser-optimization-strategy.md)\n4. **For Product Context**: See &#96;../01-core/&#96; → &#96;../../docs/internal/&#96;\n\n---\n\n**Last Updated**: 2026-02-07",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/03-platforms/01-multi-platform-strategy.md",
        "range": {

        },
        "contents": "### Phase 17: Desktop App Development\n\n**Goal**: Build native desktop apps for macOS, Windows, Linux\n\n**Steps**:\n1. **Desktop App Foundation**\n   - Choose framework (Tauri vs Electron)\n   - Set up desktop project structure\n   - Window management\n   - Menu bar integration\n\n2. **Desktop-Specific Features**\n   - Keyboard shortcuts\n   - System tray integration\n   - Media key support\n   - File system access\n   - Multi-window support\n\n3. **Desktop UI Adaptations**\n   - Desktop-optimized layouts\n   - Mouse interactions\n   - Keyboard navigation\n   - Window controls\n\n4. **Distribution**\n   - Code signing (macOS, Windows)\n   - Auto-updater setup\n   - App store submission (macOS App Store, Microsoft Store)\n   - Direct download distribution\n\n**Dependencies**: Phase 1-12 (Core app complete)\n\n---\n\n## Implementation Strategy\n\n### Parallel Development (Phases 1-13)\n**All platforms developed simultaneously**:\n- **Mobile**: React Native + Expo\n- **Desktop**: Electron/Tauri (set up in Phase 1)\n- **Web**: Next.js (existing, updated in parallel)\n\n**Each Phase**:\n- Implement feature on all three platforms\n- Test on all three platforms simultaneously\n- Ensure consistency and quality across platforms\n\n### Future Phases (14+)\n- **Phase 14**: Marketplace Platform (all platforms)\n- **Phase 15**: Social &amp; Community (all platforms)\n- **Phase 16**: Advanced Features (all platforms)\n\n---\n\n## Quality Assurance Across Platforms\n\n### Simultaneous Testing Strategy\n\n**During Each Phase**:\n- **Parallel Testing**: Test all three platforms simultaneously\n- **Consistency Checks**: Ensure UI/UX consistency across platforms\n- **Performance Monitoring**: Monitor performance on all platforms\n- **Automated Testing**: Unit, integration, E2E tests for all platforms\n- **Visual Regression**: Test visual consistency across platforms\n- **Accessibility Audits**: WCAG compliance on all platforms\n\n**Testing Workflow**:\n1. Implement feature on Platform A (Mobile)\n2. Implement same feature on Platform B (Desktop)\n3. Implement same feature on Platform C (Web)\n4. Test all three platforms simultaneously\n5. Fix inconsistencies",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/02-mobile/04-android-sdk-requirements.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 154
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 248
          }
        },
        "contents": "3. **Install Android SDK**:\n   - Open Android Studio\n   - Go to Tools &gt; SDK Manager\n   - Install Android SDK Platform 33+\n   - Install Android SDK Build-Tools\n\n4. **Verify Installation**:\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n   adb version  # Should show version number\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Recommended Setup for waQup\n\n### For Most Developers (Recommended)\n\n**Use Expo Go - No Android SDK needed**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# 1. Install dependencies\nnpm install\n\n# 2. Start Expo\nnpm run dev:mobile\n\n# 3. Scan QR code with Expo Go app\n# Done! No Android SDK required.\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### For Production Builds\n\n**Use EAS Build (Cloud) - No local Android SDK needed**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# 1. Install EAS CLI\nnpm install -g eas-cli\n\n# 2. Login to Expo\neas login\n\n# 3. Configure build\neas build:configure\n\n# 4. Build in cloud\neas build --platform android --profile production\n\n# EAS Build handles Android SDK in the cloud\n# No local Android SDK installation needed!\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Deprecation Warnings Explained\n\n### Common Deprecation Warnings\n\n1. **Node.js Version Warning**:\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n   npm warn EBADENGINE Unsupported engine\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n   - **Fixed**: Updated &#96;package.json&#96; to require Node &gt;=24.0.0\n   - **Action**: No action needed if using Node &gt;= 24.0.0\n\n2. **Package Deprecations**:\n   - Some packages may show deprecation warnings\n   - These are usually warnings, not errors\n   - Most can be safely ignored if functionality works\n\n3. **React Navigation v7**:\n   - ✅ Compatible with Expo SDK 54\n   - ✅ Requires React Native &gt;= 0.72.0 (we have 0.81.5)\n   - ✅ Uses static configuration API (newer, better TypeScript support)\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n**For waQup Development**:\n\n1. **Start with Expo Go** - No Android SDK needed\n2. **Use EAS Build for production** - No local Android SDK needed\n3. **Only install Android SDK if**:\n   - You need custom native modules\n   - You want to build locally (not recommended)\n   - You need Android Emulator for testing\n\n**Bottom Line**: You can develop the entire waQup mobile app without installing Android SDK by using Expo Go for development and EAS Build for production builds.\n\n---\n\n## References\n\n- **Expo Go**: https://docs.expo.dev/get-started/expo-go/",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "3. **Install Android SDK**:",
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Open Android Studio",
            "lineNumber": 156
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Go to Tools &gt; SDK Manager",
            "lineNumber": 157
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Install Android SDK Platform 33+",
            "lineNumber": 158
          },
          {
            "text": "   - Install Android SDK Build-Tools",
            "lineNumber": 159
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "text": "4. **Verify Installation**:",
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "text": "   adb version  # Should show version number",
            "lineNumber": 163
          },
          {
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 164
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 166
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "text": "## Recommended Setup for waQup",
            "lineNumber": 168
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "text": "### For Most Developers (Recommended)",
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "**Use Expo Go - No Android SDK needed**",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "# 1. Install dependencies",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "text": "npm install",
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "# 2. Start Expo",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "npm run dev:mobile",
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "text": "# 3. Scan QR code with Expo Go app",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "# Done! No Android SDK required.",
            "lineNumber": 182
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 183
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184
          },
          {
            "text": "### For Production Builds",
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "**Use EAS Build (Cloud) - No local Android SDK needed**",
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash",
            "lineNumber": 189
          },
          {
            "text": "# 1. Install EAS CLI",
            "lineNumber": 190
          },
          {
            "text": "npm install -g eas-cli",
            "lineNumber": 191
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192
          },
          {
            "text": "# 2. Login to Expo",
            "lineNumber": 193
          },
          {
            "text": "eas login",
            "lineNumber": 194
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195
          },
          {
            "text": "# 3. Configure build",
            "lineNumber": 196
          },
          {
            "text": "eas build:configure",
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198
          },
          {
            "text": "# 4. Build in cloud",
            "lineNumber": 199
          },
          {
            "text": "eas build --platform android --profile production",
            "lineNumber": 200
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201
          },
          {
            "text": "# EAS Build handles Android SDK in the cloud",
            "lineNumber": 202
          },
          {
            "text": "# No local Android SDK installation needed!",
            "lineNumber": 203
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 206
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "text": "## Deprecation Warnings Explained",

          {

          },

        "contents": "# Mobile-Specific Documentation\n\n**Purpose**: Mobile app development documentation (iOS &amp; Android)\n\n---\n\n## Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n02-mobile/\n├── README.md                    # This file\n├── 01-technology-stack.md      # Mobile tech stack &amp; decisions\n├── 02-architecture.md          # Mobile app architecture\n├── 03-implementation.md        # Mobile implementation guide\n└── 04-android-sdk-requirements.md  # Android SDK requirements &amp; setup\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Quick Navigation\n\n- **[01 Technology Stack](./01-technology-stack.md)** - Mobile tech decisions, packages, cost analysis\n- **[02 Architecture](./02-architecture.md)** - Mobile architecture, layers, data flow, structure\n- **[03 Implementation](./03-implementation.md)** - Mobile implementation patterns, gotchas, best practices\n- **[04 Android SDK Requirements](./04-android-sdk-requirements.md)** - Android SDK setup guide (optional for Expo Go)\n\n---\n\n## Related Documentation\n\n### Multi-Platform Strategy\nSee &#96;../03-platforms/&#96; for:\n- Multi-platform development strategy (Mobile + Web)\n- Browser optimization strategy\n\n### Core Product Docs\nSee &#96;../01-core/&#96; for:\n- Reference to main product documentation\n\n### Reference Guides\nSee &#96;../04-reference/&#96; for:\n- Context7 usage guide\n- Cursor rules guide\n\n---\n\n## Mobile-Specific Focus\n\nThis folder contains **mobile-specific** documentation:\n- React Native + Expo setup\n- Mobile navigation (React Navigation)\n- Mobile audio (expo-av)\n- Mobile-specific patterns and optimizations\n\nFor **cross-platform** or **multi-platform** strategies, see &#96;../03-platforms/&#96;.\n\n---\n\n**Last Updated**: 2026-02-07",

        "contents": "3. Implement same feature on Platform C (Web)\n4. Test all three platforms simultaneously\n5. Fix inconsistencies\n6. Verify quality standards met on all platforms\n\n**Before Release**:\n- Manual QA on all platforms simultaneously\n- Performance benchmarking across platforms\n- Cross-platform consistency verification\n- User acceptance testing on all platforms\n- Beta testing program (all platforms)\n\n**Post-Release**:\n- Crash monitoring (Sentry) for all platforms\n- Performance monitoring across platforms\n- User feedback collection (platform-specific)\n- A/B testing (future, all platforms)\n\n---\n\n## References\n\n- [Mobile Technology Stack](../02-mobile/01-technology-stack.md)\n- [Mobile Architecture](../02-mobile/02-architecture.md)\n- [Mobile Implementation](../02-mobile/03-implementation.md)\n- [Browser Optimization Strategy](./02-browser-optimization-strategy.md)\n- [Marketplace Platform](../../../docs/internal/marketplace-platform.md)\n- [Undocumented Features](../../../docs/internal/undocumented-features.md)\n\n---\n\n**Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n**Status**: 📝 Planning",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/03-platforms/02-browser-optimization-strategy.md",

        },
        "contents": "// Use Chrome's optimized audio nodes\nconst gainNode = audioContext.createGain();\nconst analyser = audioContext.createAnalyser();\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Performance Optimizations\n\n**Chrome-Specific**:\n- **V8 Optimizations**: Optimize for V8 engine\n- **JIT Compilation**: Best JavaScript performance\n- **Memory Management**: Optimize for Chrome's GC\n- **Rendering**: Optimize for Blink engine\n\n**Code Optimizations**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript\n// Optimize for V8\n// Use const/let (not var)\n// Avoid eval()\n// Use typed arrays for performance\n// Optimize hot paths for V8\n\n// Chrome-specific performance hints\n&lt;link rel=\"preconnect\" href=\"https://api.waqup.com\"&gt;\n&lt;link rel=\"dns-prefetch\" href=\"https://api.waqup.com\"&gt;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 3. PWA Features\n\n**Chrome Best Support**:\n- **Service Workers**: Full support\n- **Web App Manifest**: Complete support\n- **Offline Support**: Best implementation\n- **Background Sync**: Full support\n- **Push Notifications**: Best support\n- **Install Prompt**: Native-like experience\n\n**Chrome-Specific PWA**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n// manifest.json - Optimized for Chrome\n{\n  \"name\": \"waQup\",\n  \"short_name\": \"waQup\",\n  \"start_url\": \"/\",\n  \"display\": \"standalone\",\n  \"background_color\": \"#000000\",\n  \"theme_color\": \"#000000\",\n  \"icons\": [\n    {\n      \"src\": \"/icon-192.png\",\n      \"sizes\": \"192x192\",\n      \"type\": \"image/png\",\n      \"purpose\": \"any maskable\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"prefer_related_applications\": false\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 4. Advanced Features\n\n**Chrome-Only Features** (can use initially):\n- **WebGPU**: Advanced graphics (future)\n- **WebAssembly SIMD**: Performance optimizations\n- **WebCodecs API**: Advanced media processing\n- **File System Access API**: Direct file access\n- **Web Share API**: Native sharing\n- **Web Locks API**: Better concurrency\n\n---\n\n## User Recommendation Strategy\n\n### In-App Browser Recommendation\n\n**When to Show**:\n- On first visit (if not Chrome)\n- In settings/preferences\n- If performance issues detected\n- Optional banner/notification\n\n**Messaging**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"For the best waQup experience, we recommend using Google Chrome.\n\nChrome provides:\n✓ Faster performance\n✓ Better audio quality",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// Use Chrome's optimized audio nodes",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "const gainNode = audioContext.createGain();",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "const analyser = audioContext.createAnalyser();",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 71

          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "### 2. Performance Optimizations",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "**Chrome-Specific**:",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "- **V8 Optimizations**: Optimize for V8 engine",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "- **JIT Compilation**: Best JavaScript performance",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Memory Management**: Optimize for Chrome's GC",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Rendering**: Optimize for Blink engine",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "**Code Optimizations**:",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;javascript",

          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "### 3. PWA Features",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "**Chrome Best Support**:",

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {

          },

          {

        "contents": "import { z } from 'zod';\n\nconst schema = z.object({\n  email: z.string().email(),\n  password: z.string().min(8),\n});\n\nconst { control, handleSubmit } = useForm({\n  resolver: zodResolver(schema),\n});\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Real-Time Subscriptions\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nuseEffect(() =&gt; {\n  const channel = supabase\n    .channel('content-changes')\n    .on('postgres_changes', {\n      event: 'UPDATE',\n      schema: 'public',\n      table: 'content_items',\n      filter: &#96;user_id=eq.${userId}&#96;,\n    }, (payload) =&gt; {\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries(['content']);\n    })\n    .subscribe();\n\n  return () =&gt; {\n    supabase.removeChannel(channel);\n  };\n}, [userId]);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Common Gotchas\n\n### 1. AsyncStorage Keys\n- Use consistent naming\n- Handle migration if keys change\n\n### 2. Navigation State\n- Don't mutate directly\n- Use navigation actions\n\n### 3. Audio Session\n- Configure properly in app.json\n- Handle interruptions gracefully\n\n### 4. Image Loading\n- Optimize images (WebP when possible)\n- Use proper formats\n\n### 5. Bundle Size\n- Monitor size\n- Use code splitting\n- Remove unused deps\n\n---\n\n## Platform-Specific\n\n### iOS\n- Configure Info.plist properly\n- Handle background audio\n- Test on different iOS versions\n- Follow App Store guidelines\n\n### Android\n- Configure permissions properly\n- Handle back button\n- Test on different Android versions\n- Follow Play Store guidelines\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\ntry {\n  await createContent(data);\n} catch (error) {\n  if (error.code === 'NETWORK_ERROR') {\n    showError('No internet connection');\n  } else {\n    showError('Something went wrong. Please try again.');\n  }\n  logError(error);\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Testing\n\n### Unit Tests\n- Utilities\n- Components\n- Hooks\n- Services\n\n### Integration Tests\n- Feature flows\n- Navigation\n- State management\n\n### E2E Tests\n- Critical flows\n- Real devices\n- Offline scenarios\n\n**Target**: &gt;80% code coverage\n\n---\n\n## Performance Tips\n\n1. **Lazy Load Screens**: Use &#96;React.lazy()&#96;\n2. **Memo Expensive Components**: Use &#96;React.memo()&#96;\n3. **Optimize Images**: WebP, compression\n4. **Cache Aggressively**: React Query + local storage",

            "lineNumber": 203
          },
          {
            "text": "1. **Lazy Load Screens**: Use &#96;React.lazy()&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 204
          },
          {
            "text": "2. **Memo Expensive Components**: Use &#96;React.memo()&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "text": "3. **Optimize Images**: WebP, compression",

          },

            "text": "4. **Cache Aggressively**: React Query + local storage",

          }
        ]

        "contents": "- BrowserStack\n- Sauce Labs\n- Local testing on each browser\n\n---\n\n## Performance Targets (Chrome)\n\n### Chrome-Specific Targets\n\n**Performance**:\n- **First Load**: &lt; 2s (Chrome's fast connection)\n- **Subsequent Loads**: &lt; 500ms (Service Worker caching)\n- **Lighthouse Score**: &gt; 95 (Chrome optimized)\n- **Audio Latency**: &lt; 20ms (Chrome's Web Audio)\n- **Animation FPS**: 60fps (Chrome's rendering)\n\n**Optimizations**:\n- V8 engine optimizations\n- Chrome's rendering optimizations\n- Chrome's caching strategies\n- Chrome's compression\n\n---\n\n## Migration Path to Other Browsers\n\n### Step 1: Add Browser Detection\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// Add browser detection utility\nexport function detectBrowser() { /* ... */ }\nexport function getBrowserFeatures() { /* ... */ }\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 2: Add Feature Flags\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\n// Feature flags based on browser\nconst features = getBrowserFeatures();\n\nif (features.webAudio) {\n  // Use Web Audio API\n} else {\n  // Fallback to HTML5 Audio\n}\n\nif (features.serviceWorkers) {\n  // Use Service Workers\n} else {\n  // Fallback to basic caching\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 3: Browser-Specific Optimizations\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nconst browser = detectBrowser();\n\nswitch (browser) {\n  case Browser.CHROME:\n    // Chrome optimizations\n    break;\n  case Browser.SAFARI:\n    // Safari optimizations\n    break;\n  case Browser.FIREFOX:\n    // Firefox optimizations\n    break;\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 4: Cross-Browser Testing\n\n- Test on each browser\n- Fix browser-specific issues\n- Add fallbacks where needed\n- Update browser recommendation\n\n---\n\n## User Communication\n\n### Browser Recommendation UI\n\n**Design**:\n- Non-intrusive banner\n- Dismissible\n- Clear benefits\n- Download link\n- \"Continue Anyway\" option\n\n**Copy**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\"For the best waQup experience, use Google Chrome.\n\n✓ Faster performance\n✓ Better audio quality  \n✓ Full feature support\n✓ Offline capabilities\n\n[Get Chrome] [Continue with Current Browser]\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Settings Option\n\n**Add to Settings**:\n- Browser recommendation toggle\n- Browser detection info\n- Supported browsers list\n- Feature availability by browser\n\n---\n\n## Benefits of Chrome-First Approach",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/04-reference/01-showcase-access.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 64
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Component Showcase Access\n\n**Purpose**: Single source of truth for accessing the component showcase on Web and Mobile.\n\n---\n\n## Web (Next.js)\n\n- **URL (local)**: &#96;http://localhost:3000/showcase&#96;\n- **URL (production)**: &#96;https://your-domain.com/showcase&#96;\n- The route is not linked in main navigation; use the URL directly.\n\n---\n\n## Mobile (React Native + Expo)\n\n### Deep links\n\n- **Custom scheme (dev)**: &#96;waqup://showcase&#96;\n- **Universal link (prod)**: &#96;https://waqup.app/showcase&#96;\n\n### Testing deep links\n\n**iOS Simulator**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nxcrun simctl openurl booted \"waqup://showcase\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Android Emulator**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nadb shell am start -W -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d \"waqup://showcase\" com.waqup.app\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Expo Go**:\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnpx uri-scheme open waqup://showcase --ios\nnpx uri-scheme open waqup://showcase --android\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Programmatic navigation (dev)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;typescript\nimport { useNavigation } from '@react-navigation/native';\nconst navigation = useNavigation();\nnavigation.navigate('Showcase');\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Notes\n\n- Showcase is hidden from normal navigation (tabs/menus).\n- Access is via direct URL or deep link for development and testing.\n- In production you may add auth or remove the route.\n\n---\n\n## Route reference\n\n| Platform | Route / link | Notes |\n|----------|--------------|--------|\n| Web | &#96;/showcase&#96; | URL only |\n| Mobile | &#96;waqup://showcase&#96; | Deep link |\n| Mobile | &#96;waqup://login&#96;, &#96;waqup://signup&#96;, &#96;waqup://home&#96;, &#96;waqup://library&#96;, &#96;waqup://create&#96;, &#96;waqup://profile&#96; | Auth required for home/library/create/profile |",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./docs/02-mobile/02-architecture.md",

        "contents": "- Handle interruptions gracefully\n- Cache audio files locally\n- Stream from CDN\n\n### Performance\n- Lazy load screens\n- Optimize images\n- Code splitting\n- Memoization\n\n---\n\n## Integration Points\n\n### Supabase\n- **Database**: PostgreSQL queries\n- **Auth**: Supabase Auth v2 with PKCE\n- **Storage**: Audio file upload/download\n- **Realtime**: Live updates\n\n### External APIs\n- **ElevenLabs**: Voice cloning, TTS\n- **OpenAI**: Conversations, script generation\n- **Stripe**: Payments, subscriptions\n\n---\n\n## Security\n\n- Supabase Auth v2 with PKCE\n- Secure token storage (Keychain/Keystore)\n- HTTPS only\n- RLS policies\n- GDPR compliant\n\n---\n\n## Performance Targets\n\n- App launch: &lt; 2s\n- Screen transition: &lt; 300ms\n- API response: &lt; 500ms\n- Audio playback start: &lt; 1s\n- Bundle size: &lt; 50MB\n\n---\n\n## References\n\n- **Full Architecture**: See &#96;../../../docs/internal/05-system-architecture.md&#96;\n- **Implementation**: See [03-implementation.md](./03-implementation.md)\n- **Multi-Platform Strategy**: See &#96;../03-platforms/01-multi-platform-strategy.md&#96;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "- Handle interruptions gracefully",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "- Cache audio files locally",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "- Stream from CDN",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "text": "### Performance",
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "- Lazy load screens",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "text": "- Optimize images",
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "- Code splitting",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "- Memoization",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "text": "## Integration Points",
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "### Supabase",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Database**: PostgreSQL queries",
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Auth**: Supabase Auth v2 with PKCE",
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Storage**: Audio file upload/download",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Realtime**: Live updates",

          },

          },

          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },

            "text": "---",
            "lineNumber": 119
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "text": "## Security",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "text": "- Supabase Auth v2 with PKCE",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {

        "contents": "# Android SDK Requirements - Expo Development\n\n**Status**: ✅ Current | **Last Updated**: 2026-02-07\n\n## Quick Answer\n\n**Do you need Android SDK?**\n\n- ✅ **NO** - For development with Expo Go (recommended for most developers)\n- ⚠️ **YES** - For building custom development builds or production APKs\n\n---\n\n## Development Options\n\n### Option 1: Expo Go (No Android SDK Required) ✅ Recommended\n\n**Best for**: Most developers, especially when starting out\n\n**What you need**:\n- Node.js &gt;= 24.0.0\n- npm 10.0.0+\n- Expo CLI\n- Expo Go app on your phone (iOS/Android)\n\n**How it works**:\n1. Run &#96;npm run dev:mobile&#96; or &#96;expo start&#96;\n2. Scan QR code with Expo Go app\n3. App runs on your phone instantly\n4. Hot reload works automatically\n\n**Advantages**:\n- ✅ No Android SDK installation needed\n- ✅ No Android Studio needed\n- ✅ Works on any device with Expo Go\n- ✅ Fastest development setup\n- ✅ No build times\n\n**Limitations**:\n- ⚠️ Can't use custom native modules not included in Expo Go\n- ⚠️ Some advanced native features may not be available\n\n---\n\n### Option 2: Development Builds (Android SDK Required)\n\n**Best for**: When you need custom native modules or advanced features\n\n**What you need**:\n- Everything from Option 1, PLUS:\n- Android Studio (latest)\n- Android SDK (API 33+)\n- Java Development Kit (JDK) 17+\n- Android Emulator or physical device\n\n**How it works**:\n1. Build custom development build: &#96;eas build --profile development --platform android&#96;\n2. Install on device/emulator\n3. Run &#96;expo start --dev-client&#96;\n4. App runs with your custom native code\n\n**Advantages**:\n- ✅ Full access to all native modules\n- ✅ Can use any Expo module or custom native code\n- ✅ Production-like environment\n\n**Disadvantages**:\n- ❌ Requires Android SDK setup\n- ❌ Longer initial setup time\n- ❌ Build times for each update\n\n---\n\n### Option 3: Production Builds (Android SDK Required)\n\n**Best for**: Building APKs/IPAs for app stores\n\n**What you need**:\n- Everything from Option 2\n- EAS Build account (or local build setup)",

        "contents": "export default function HomePage() {\n  const { theme } = useTheme();\n  const colors = theme.colors;\n\n  return (\n    &lt;PageShell intensity=\"medium\"&gt;\n      &lt;PageContent&gt;\n          {/* Header */}\n          &lt;div style={{ marginBottom: spacing.xl }}&gt;\n            &lt;Typography variant=\"h1\" style={{ marginBottom: spacing.sm, color: colors.text.primary }}&gt;\n              Welcome to Your Sanctuary\n            &lt;/Typography&gt;\n            &lt;Typography variant=\"body\" style={{ color: colors.text.secondary }}&gt;\n              Your space for transformation and growth\n            &lt;/Typography&gt;\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n\n          {/* Quick Actions */}\n          &lt;div\n            style={{\n              display: 'grid',\n              gridTemplateColumns: &#96;repeat(auto-fit, minmax(${GRID_CARD_MIN}, 1fr))&#96;,\n              gap: spacing.lg,\n              marginBottom: spacing.xl,\n            }}\n          &gt;\n            {QUICK_ACTIONS.map((action, index) =&gt; {\n              // First two: more opaque (darker), last one: more transparent (lighter)\n              const isOpaque = index &lt; 2;\n              const cardBackground = isOpaque ? colors.glass.opaque : colors.glass.transparent;\n              const textColor = isOpaque ? colors.text.onDark : colors.text.primary;\n              const secondaryTextColor = isOpaque ? colors.text.onDark : colors.text.secondary;\n              \n              return (\n                &lt;Link key={action.name} href={action.href} style={{ textDecoration: 'none' }}&gt;\n                  &lt;Card\n                    variant=\"elevated\"\n                    pressable\n                    style={{\n                      padding: spacing.lg,\n                      height: '100%',\n                      transition: 'all 0.3s ease',\n                      cursor: 'pointer'\n            }\n}",

        "contents": "export default function CreatePage() {\n\n          &lt;div style={{ marginBottom: spacing.xl }}&gt;\n            &lt;Typography variant=\"h1\" style={{ marginBottom: spacing.sm, color: colors.text.primary }}&gt;\n              Create New Content\n            &lt;/Typography&gt;\n            &lt;Typography variant=\"body\" style={{ color: colors.text.secondary }}&gt;\n              Choose the type of content you want to create\n            &lt;/Typography&gt;\n          &lt;/div&gt;\n\n          {/* Content Type Cards */}\n          &lt;div\n            style={{\n              display: 'grid',\n              gridTemplateColumns: &#96;repeat(auto-fit, minmax(${GRID_CARD_MIN}, 1fr))&#96;,\n              gap: spacing.xl,\n            }}\n          &gt;\n            {CONTENT_TYPES.map((type, index) =&gt; {\n              const IconComponent = type.icon;\n              // First card: more opaque (darker), others: more transparent (lighter)\n              const isOpaque = index === 0;\n              const cardBackground = isOpaque ? colors.glass.opaque : colors.glass.transparent;\n              const textColor = isOpaque ? colors.text.onDark : colors.text.primary;\n              const secondaryTextColor = isOpaque ? colors.text.onDark : colors.text.secondary;\n              \n              return (\n                &lt;Link key={type.name} href={type.href} style={{ textDecoration: 'none' }}&gt;\n                  &lt;Card\n                    variant=\"elevated\"\n                    pressable\n                    style={{\n                      padding: spacing.xxl,\n                      height: '100%',\n                      minHeight: '300px',\n                      transition: 'all 0.3s ease'\n            }\n}",
